articles.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise (25 minute read)
Making budget models punch above their weight with a smart harness (18 minute read)
How We Made Cloud Browsers 3x Cheaper and Faster (17 minute read)
articles.summary: Browser Use's cloud browsers have been optimized to start quickly, remain isolated, and maintain low costs, now operating at $0.02 per hour with sessions starting in under a second. This was done by using Firecracker for lightweight virtual machines while implementing a control plane for dynamic scaling.

articles.title: The Dialogue Dividend (6 minute read)
articles.summary: Conversations with others often lead to greater problem-solving and understanding than solitary thinking can provide as they introduce a feedback loop that refines thoughts in real-time.

articles.title: But yak shaving is fun (8 minute read)
articles.summary: Yak shaving refers to the process of engaging in a series of tangential tasks that divert from the original goal, showing how building projects from scratch can lead to unexpected challenges, discoveries, and joy.

articles.title: Introducing eve (12 minute read)
articles.summary: eve is an open-source agent framework that makes building, running, and scaling agents easier by providing built-in production capabilities like durable execution, sandboxed computing, and human-in-the-loop approvals. With a clear structure, agents can be defined through a directory of files that dictate their components, actions, and connections.

articles.title: Lore (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: Lore is an open source version control system designed for scalability, particularly for projects with large binary assets like games. It has a centralized, content-addressed architecture that allows for efficient data storage and retrieval, with an emphasis on immutable revisions and low-overhead branching.

articles.title: Building TREX: Code execution and artifact generation for AI code review (9 minute read)
articles.summary: TREX is a code review tool that integrates code execution with AI-driven testing to identify bugs that may not be apparent in static analysis alone. It uses a multi-agent architecture, where a Greptile reviewer orchestrates specialized TREX agents that run tests in isolated environments, providing artifacts such as screenshots and logs to verify findings.

articles.title: Image compression (35 minute read)
articles.summary: Image compression techniques, including lossy and lossless methods, exploit characteristics of human visual perception to reduce file sizes. Formats like JPEG use a combination of chroma subsampling, discrete cosine transforms, and quantization for compression without noticeable quality loss, while GIF uses indexed color palettes and Lempel-Ziv-Welch algorithms to store limited color data.

articles.title: Brain the Size of a Planet: Are LLMs Thonking too Hard? (26 minute read)
The founder's playbook: Building an AI-native startup (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Anthropic has released a playbook of practical exercises and agentic workflows to navigate every stage of the business lifecycle for AI-native startups.

articles.title: mac-ocr (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: mac-ocr is a macOS command-line tool that uses Apple's Vision framework locally to recognize text from images and PDFs and create searchable PDFs.

articles.title: Can gzip be a language model? (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Gzip can be used as a language model through compression techniques that predict and generate text continuations by scoring potential outputs based on how well they compress with a given context.

articles.title: Exa Agent (5 minute read)
articles.summary: Exa Agent is a new API that combines frontier language models with optimized web search tools to provide high-performance research, list-building, and data enrichment capabilities.
